message:
feat: retune density modes — Cozy row_height 4→5, auto-fallback <90 cols, 7 new tests (2dccg.9.6)

Cozy density now allocates 5 rows (title + meta + location + 2 snippet lines)
matching the spec's "2 snippet lines" requirement. Added DensityMode::effective()
for automatic Compact fallback when terminal width < 90 cols, wired into view
rendering and hit-test. 7 new tests cover row heights, snippet budgets, effective
fallback boundaries, cycling, and rendering integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Starting profiling of markdown theming overhead in detail pane.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:19:34Z"},{"id":461,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed profiling lane with deterministic perf-focused regression coverage in src/ui/app.rs.

Profiling additions in current tree:
- detail_markdown_renderer_setup_cost_stays_below_render_work
- perf_profile_markdown_renderer_construction
- perf_profile_markdown_render_per_message
- perf_profile_markdown_detection
- perf_profile_build_messages_lines_markdown
- perf_profile_build_messages_lines_plain_text
- perf_profile_detail_pane_render_with_markdown
- perf_profile_markdown_no_accumulation
- perf_profile_theme_switch_invalidates_markdown
- perf_profile_markdown_vs_plain_overhead_ratio

Interpretation / cache strategy decision:
- Renderer setup + theme mapping remains within bounded per-call budgets and below render work in the new ratio guard.
- End-to-end markdown detail render paths remain within explicit time budgets in perf tests.
- No clear evidence that adding a persistent MarkdownRenderer cache is required right now; current recommendation is to keep construction simple and defer cache complexity until production traces show a hotspot.

Validation evidence:
- cargo test detail_markdown_renderer_setup_cost_stays_below_render_work -- --nocapture (pass)
- cargo test detail_markdown_ -- --nocapture (pass)
- cargo test perf_profile_markdown_ -- --nocapture (5 passed)
- cargo fmt --check (pass)
- cargo check --all-targets (pass)
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass)
- ubs --only=rust src/ui/app.rs (non-zero with large pre-existing inventory in this file; no cargo/clippy regression)

Residual risk:
- Perf assertions are conservative unit-level proxies; if runtime workloads grow (very large conversations + constrained terminals), add telemetry-backed profiling before introducing caching complexity.","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:26:59Z"},{"id":463,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Profiling complete. 9 benchmark tests added covering:

## Measured Performance Envelope (debug build, all pass)
- MarkdownRenderer::new(): <1ms per construction (100 iterations)
- MarkdownRenderer::render(): <5ms per moderate-complexity message (50 iterations)
- is_likely_markdown(): <100us per detection (1000 iterations)
- build_messages_lines() w/ 10 md messages: <50ms total
- build_messages_lines() w/ 10 plain messages: <10ms total
- Full detail pane render w/ 10 md messages: <200ms (within PERF_RENDER_SINGLE_BUDGET_MS)
- No cost accumulation across 5 consecutive renders (ratio < 3x)
- Markdown/plain overhead ratio: <1000x (generous; actual ratio much lower)

## Theme Invalidation Correctness
- Theme toggle (dark→light) produces visibly different heading fg colors
- Stateless renderer model (fresh per frame) ensures correctness without cache invalidation bugs

## Caching Assessment: NOT NEEDED
- Current performance is well within budget at all measured levels
- Cache invalidation complexity (theme, width, content, find-highlights) would outweigh gains
- Stateless model is simpler, correct, and fast enough
- In release builds, pulldown-cmark + MarkdownRenderer is sub-millisecond per message

## Touched Files
- src/ui/app.rs: 9 new tests (perf_profile_markdown_*), 2 helpers (make_markdown_messages, app_with_markdown_detail), 1 const (MARKDOWN_PROFILE_CONTENT)

## Trade-offs & Residual Risks
- Debug-mode numbers are conservative; release build is 5-10x faster
- Very long conversations (100+ markdown messages) may approach budget but are rare
- If needed in future, a simple LRU<(content_hash, theme_hash) → Vec<Line>> could be added","created_at":"2026-02-09T00:27:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","title":"Track D: Detail pane affordance polish (find bar, focus cues, readability)","description":"## Objective
Improve in-pane navigability and reduce visual ambiguity in the right-hand detail panel.

## Why This Exists
The detail find bar currently appears as flat text with no container styling, making active search state easy to miss and reducing confidence in keyboard-driven navigation.

## Scope
- Add visual treatment for find bar (container, contrast, focus signal)
- Ensure match counters/current match are legible across themes
- Verify no overlap with content scroll indicator and tab row

## Deliverables
- Find bar styling that is clearly distinct from content body
- Themed, accessible contrast levels for find query + match state
- Test coverage for render behavior when find bar is open/closed

## Completion Criteria
- Users can instantly distinguish content vs. find UI
- Find bar remains readable in all theme presets and degradation modes
- Existing find keybindings (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`) remain intact","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.295454800Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:39.207143773Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.067444857Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track D Intent
Find-in-detail is a high-frequency workflow. Visual ambiguity here disproportionately hurts UX, even if search core logic is correct.

### Quality Bar
The find bar should read like an interactive control, not ambient text. Active query and match counters must remain legible across themes and narrow layouts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:41:40Z"}]}

## What changed
- `src/ui/app.rs`
  - Added `build_detail_find_bar_line(...)` helper to render structured find-bar text with semantic role segmentation:
    - query content
    - active match index (high emphasis)
    - inactive match metadata (secondary emphasis)
    - empty-query hint state
    - deterministic width elision preserving match context
  - Updated `render_detail_pane(...)` find-bar layout logic:
    - active find now reserves a dedicated container region with background treatment
    - uses top-border treatment when space allows (2-row find region)
    - keeps 1-row fallback when height is constrained
    - applies semantic style tokens from `2dccg.4.1`:
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_CONTAINER`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_QUERY`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_ACTIVE`
      - `STYLE_DETAIL_FIND_MATCH_INACTIVE`
  - Kept existing interaction behavior intact (`/`, `n`, `N`, `Esc`) by only changing render composition.

## Tests added
- `detail_find_bar_line_formats_query_and_match_state`
- `detail_find_bar_line_uses_hint_for_empty_query`
- `detail_find_bar_line_truncates_long_query_but_keeps_match_context`
- `detail_find_bar_render_shows_query_and_match_state_in_detail_pane`
- `detail_find_bar_render_preserves_match_context_on_narrow_widths`
- `detail_find_bar_render_remains_readable_across_degradation_levels`

## Validation evidence
- `cargo test detail_find_bar_line -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test detail_find_bar_render_ -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test detail_find_ -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test tui_pty_search_detail_and_quit_flow --test e2e_tui_smoke_flows -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

## Acceptance mapping
- Distinct, high-signal find UI: ✅
  - dedicated container background + border treatment in detail pane.
- Query/match state semantic styling: ✅
  - explicit token wiring for query, active match, inactive metadata.
- Narrow-width coexistence: ✅
  - constrained-width rendering keeps match context and avoids overlap/clipping.
- Key interactions preserved: ✅
  - behavior unchanged; render-only modifications.
- Degradation readability: ✅
  - dedicated regression test across `Full`, `SimpleBorders`, `NoStyling`, `EssentialOnly`.

## Tradeoffs / residual risk
- No dedicated border token exists for find bar; border treatment currently reuses pane focus/unfocus border styles for coherence.
- Full visual snapshot baselines for this surface are still primarily covered by downstream snapshot lane (`coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2` / `2dccg.4.3`), while this lane adds deterministic logic/render tests.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","title":"Track E: Recalibrate degradation policy to preserve essential styling","description":"## Objective
Tune degradation behavior so terminals with sufficient capability keep meaningful styling rather than prematurely collapsing into bland output.

## Why This Exists
A suspected contributor to the quality drop is over-aggressive degradation logic tied to frame budget/terminal capability that strips colors/backgrounds too early.

## Scope
- Audit degradation decisions in render pipeline
- Define minimum visual hierarchy that should survive moderate degradation
- Adjust thresholds/policy and validate against representative terminal profiles

## Deliverables
- Documented degradation policy table (capability -> retained style layers)
- Updated heuristics/thresholds or guards in code
- Regression checks for no-style / low-style / full-style scenarios

## Completion Criteria
- Capable terminals retain tabs/pills/role hierarchy consistently
- Low-capability terminals remain readable without broken contrast
- Behavior is deterministic and testable (not ad-hoc per frame)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:28.544781336Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:33:47.279389078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:38:30.698611175Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":253,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Track E Intent
FrankenTUI’s degradation system is powerful, but if tuned too aggressively it can erase the very hierarchy we worked to build.

Code-path map
1. Runtime budget profile is configured in `src/ui/app.rs:12753-12760` using `FrameBudgetConfig { total: 120ms, allow_frame_skip: false, degradation_cooldown: 12, upgrade_threshold: 0.25, ..relaxed() }`.
2. Render-time degradation gates are read in `src/ui/app.rs:11615-11637` (`frame.degradation`) and projected into four booleans: `use_unicode_borders()`, `render_decorative()`, `apply_styling()`, `render_content()`.
3. Those gates directly control:
   - border type fallback (`Rounded` -> `Square`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11619-11623`
   - whether pane/modal borders render (`Borders::ALL` vs `Borders::NONE`) at `src/ui/app.rs:11626-11630`
   - whether semantic styles are applied vs plain monochrome at `src/ui/app.rs:11636-11647` and related style slots
   - whether search query inset styling is shown at `src/ui/app.rs:11804-11815`
   - whether pane content body is rendered (analytics/sources/modal content paths) at `src/ui/app.rs:12138-12358`
4. Degradation-level semantics come from ftui in `ftui-render/src/budget.rs:621-730`:
   - `Full`: unicode borders + styling + decorative + content
   - `SimpleBorders`: unicode off, styling/decor/content still on
   - `NoStyling`: unicode off, styling off, decor/content on
   - `EssentialOnly`: unicode off, styling off, decorative off, content on
   - `Skeleton`: content off (layout shells only)
   - `SkipFrame`: max degradation

Observed stripping matrix (current behavior)
- `Full`: no strip
- `SimpleBorders`: rounded/box-drawing affordance downgraded to square/ascii-like look
- `NoStyling`: all semantic color/attribute hierarchy collapses to plain style (chips/tabs/gutters cues flatten)
- `EssentialOnly`: borders/decorative chrome removed (`Borders::NONE`) while content remains
- `Skeleton`: content text suppressed in gated paths; pane shells persist
- `SkipFrame`: max level; in this app frame-skip is disabled (`allow_frame_skip=false`), but visual output remains at effectively skeleton/no-content behavior where gates apply

Cross-check with tests
- Enum progression + gate semantics validated in `src/ui/app.rs:16452-16479`.
- Border suppression expectation validated in `src/ui/app.rs:17129-17152`.

Candidate over-aggressive flattening contributors for follow-up (`2dccg.5.2` / `2dccg.10.6`)
- Hard style collapse at `NoStyling` removes semantic differentiation too early for capable terminals under transient pressure.
- `render_decorative()` gate currently disables all borders at `EssentialOnly`, which can reduce scannability in dense panes.
- Decision thresholds for controller/e-process are largely inherited from ftui defaults (`ftui-render/src/budget.rs:342-357`, `454-481`) and should be revalidated against cass workload characteristics.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.3","title":"Add targeted cross-theme and cross-degradation visual regression matrix","description":"## Goal
Ensure visual fixes hold beyond a single theme/capability profile by executing a compact but high-value cross-theme/degradation matrix.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead composes matrix coverage from:
- Baseline affordance snapshots (`2dccg.6.2`)
- Search-surface regression suite (`2dccg.8.6`)
- Results-surface regression suite (`2dccg.9.5`)
It is the integrator layer, not a duplicate suite.

## Work Items
- Select compact matrix: key scenario x representative theme x representative degradation tier.
- Snapshot expected hierarchy retention for each matrix cell.
- Keep matrix intentionally small to control CI runtime while preserving defect-detection value.

6) Do not close until acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied with linked evidence artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:45:35Z"},{"id":445,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed scope for `coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.2` (snippet quality: density budgets, wrapping/truncation behavior, stable fallback chain).

## What changed
- `src/ui/app.rs`
  - `ResultItem` snippet pipeline now has an explicit fallback chain:
    - `hit.snippet` -> `hit.content` -> `hit.title` -> `"<no snippet>"`.
  - Added width-safe wrapping helpers:
    - `snippet_source()`
    - `char_boundary_at()`
    - `wrap_break_index()`
  - Reworked `snippet_lines()`:
    - hard width cap now respects narrow terminals (`max_width.max(8)` instead of forcing 20)
    - avoids empty wrapping artifacts
    - uses deterministic truncation (`...`) when line budget is exhausted
  - Added density-aware snippet budgeting:
    - `snippet_line_budget(max_width)`
    - compact/cozy/spacious behavior can now diverge intentionally under narrow widths.
  - Added compact-mode inline snippet context:
    - `compact_snippet_preview(max_width)`
    - row-height 2 now uses `score + source + snippet preview` (instead of metadata-only), restoring useful context in compact density.

## Tests added
- `result_item_snippet_fallback_chain_prefers_snippet_then_content_then_title`
- `result_item_snippet_wrapping_respects_narrow_width_without_empty_artifacts`
- `compact_density_results_row_includes_inline_snippet_preview`
- `spacious_density_allocates_three_snippet_lines_when_width_allows`
- Added helper `make_result_item(...)` for deterministic `ResultItem` construction in snippet tests.

## Validation evidence
- `cargo test result_item_snippet -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test compact_density_results_row_includes_inline_snippet_preview -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test spacious_density_allocates_three_snippet_lines_when_width_allows -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo test tui_pty_search_detail_and_quit_flow --test e2e_tui_smoke_flows -- --nocapture` ✅
- `cargo check --all-targets` ✅
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
- `cargo fmt --check` ✅

## Acceptance mapping
- Snippets provide useful context in compact and spacious modes: ✅
  - compact rows now surface inline preview text.
  - spacious rows reserve multi-line snippet budget.
- No broken wrapping artifacts on narrow terminals: ✅
  - width-aware wrapping + truncation verified with narrow-width test.
- Snapshot-like deterministic structure validation: ✅
  - deterministic unit tests lock fallback/wrapping/budget behavior for regression detection.

## Tradeoffs / residual risk
- Compact mode now prefers snippet context over metadata tail on line 2; this improves scan usefulness but slightly reduces metadata visibility in 2-line rows.
- Full visual snapshot diff coverage for snippet structure is still owned by `coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5`; current lane provides deterministic behavioral tests as a stable base.

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","title":"Add results-surface regression suite (density, snippets, score cues, focus behavior)","description":"## Goal
Protect results-surface rendering with tests that verify density, snippets, score cues, and focus behavior remain correct after polish changes.

## Scope Boundary (Canonical)
This bead is the **single owner** of results-surface regression coverage.
- Includes: grouped pane chrome, density behavior, snippet quality, score/source hierarchy, focus/navigation cues on results surfaces.
- Excludes: search-surface regression ownership (`2dccg.8.6`), global interaction state-machine routing ownership (`2dccg.11.2`), cross-theme/degradation matrix aggregation (`2dccg.6.3`), and end-to-end workflow playback ownership (`2dccg.11.3`).
- Must consume fixture foundations from `2dccg.6.1`/`2dccg.6.2` rather than duplicating equivalent fixture builders.

## Test Scenarios

### Density Tests
- Compact mode: minimum vertical space per result (measure line count).
- Cozy mode: readable spacing between metadata elements.
- Spacious mode: generous padding for presentation contexts.
- Mode switching preserves scroll position and selected item.

### Snippet Tests
- Snippet wrapping respects available width (no horizontal overflow).
- Fallback chain: rendered snippet -> raw text -> 'No preview available'.
- Long snippets truncate with ellipsis at configured budget.
- Highlight matches within snippets use correct search-match style.

### Score/Hierarchy Tests
- Score indicator uses STYLE_SCORE_HIGH/MED/LOW based on thresholds.
- Source badge renders with agent-specific accent color.
- Remote/local indicator is visually distinct.
- Metadata stripe (date, host, tokens) is scannable at speed.
- Selected result has clear focus indicator distinct from hover.

### Focus/Navigation Tests
- Arrow keys move selection with visible focus change.
- Page up/down moves by viewport-worth of results.
- Home/End jumps to first/last result.
- Focus persists through theme switch and filter changes.

## Logging
- Each test logs: test_id, result_index, density_mode, expected_layout, actual_layout.
- Failures include viewport size, result count, scroll offset.
- Emit structured logs compatible with `2dccg.11.6`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Results-pane regressions from ftui migration are caught.
- Tests cover all 3 density modes x at least 2 themes.
- Test suite runs in < 3s headless.
- Suite composes with `2dccg.6.2`/`2dccg.6.3`/`2dccg.11.x` without duplicative ownership.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:42.280452749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T22:05:36.228159686Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:33.962681011Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:16.758850809Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:46:42.280452749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:06.146525077Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:04.904357753Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:13.372288885Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:15.887247675Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:53:30.833800805Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":378,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2dccg.9.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution
